pdn.desc: Draft Plan of Subdivision for 5 lots.

The Official Plan and Zoning By-law Amendment application proposes the redevelopment of the site to permit three buildings ranging in height between 39 and 49-storeys. The application proposes 97,921 square metres of gross floor area (GFA), including 91,425 square metres of residential GFA, 6,009 square metres of office space, and 487 square metres of retail space. A total of 1,364 residential units are proposed.
3 blocks

Official Plan and Zoning By-law Amendment applications to facilitate redevelopment at 200 University Avenue for a 35-storey addition above the existing 16-storey building for an overall height of 51-storeys. The proposal consists of 35,316 square metres of residential gross floor area containing 478 residential units, 204 square metres of retail space, and 13,052 square metres of office space, with 98 vehicular parking spaces and 548 bicycle spaces.

The proposal is for two towers of 20 and 34 storeys, connected by a 6 storey based podium, aligning with the existing and new public streets with active retail space at grade.  Also contemplates the Gunns Road extension as well as new parkland.

The proposal contemplates the development of the subject site with two mixed-use buildings consisting of commercial, retail and residential uses framing the street edge and providing room for a parkland conveyance that will serve as an expansion to the parkland planned to the south. The proposal will incorporate two towers of 23 and 37 storeys, each with 6 storey podiums, aligning the Union Street public street with active retail space at grade.

Proposed St. Clair - Old Weston GO station (Smart Track) by Metrolinx. Proposal comprises:- 3 entrance buildings: main entrance located at south end of site adjacent to Union St. Seconary entrance at north end of sit adjacent to Union St. Third entrance located on west side of rail corridor near planned extension of Gunns Rd.- Increase from three tracks to four mainline tracks- Three platforms to serve all four Metrolinx tracks, with canopies over at least 85% of the platformlength, enclosures at the top of stairs and elevators, passenger shelters, and all amenities such aslighting, public address, digital and static signage, and snowmelt.- Two tunnels to connect each of the platforms to a station building on the east side of the rail corridor.- Corridor station facilities including six-bay bus loop to accommodate TTC vehicle and Wheel Trans, aplaza, sidewalk, MUP and circulation space to accommodate all pedestrian and cycling activity,secure and covered cycling facilities, as well as landscaping, street furniture and plantings.

The proposal contemplates the development of the subject site with a mixed-use building consisting of retail and residential uses central to the property and allowing sufficient land to be conveyed for the Gunns Road extension as well as new parkland. The building will incorporate two towers of 20 and 34 storeys, connected by a 6 storey based podium, aligning with the existing and new public streets with active retail space at grade.

The redevelopment proposes a 64-storey mixed-use building with a 12-storey office podium and is inclusive of four levels of below-grade parking with a total of 171 parking spaces. The redevelopment proposes 704 dwelling units, resulting in a total residential gross floor area (GFA) of 46,683 sq. m., a total of 24,919 sq. m. of office GFA, and a total of 460 sq. m of retail GFA which results in a total Floor Space Index (FSI) of 21.65, pursuant to GFA calculations under By-law 569-2013.
Zoning By-law Amendment application to permit a 64-storey mixed-use building, containing 704 dwelling units, 25,100 square metres of office and 600 square metres of retail gross floor area.

The proposal is for two towers of 23 and 37 storeys, each with 6 storey podiums, with commercial, retail and residential uses framing the street edge and a park, expanding parkland planned to the south.

Official Plan Amendment proposing redevelopment of the Hearn Power Generating Station. The site is subject to the Port Lands Area Specific Policies of the City's Central Waterfront Secondary Plan, which envisions the Hearn as a catalytic destination for cultural, sport, entertainment, recreational and institutional uses within a revitalized iconic heritage building. The Hearn is surrounded by power generation and distribution infrastructure, port and heavy industrial uses and natural areas. Residential uses are not currently permitted. The application proposes 739,567 square metres of Gross Floor Area (GFA) including adaptive reuse of the existing power station building and development of seven new buildings with tower elements across the site.  A mix of retail, commercial and other non-residential uses are proposed, together with 8,400 residential units and 2,522 vehicle parking spaces.
